message:
Search/schema: upgrade to v4 with edge n-gram prefix fields and preview

Add three new fields to the Tantivy schema to enable efficient
search-as-you-type prefix matching and faster snippet generation:

- title_prefix: edge n-grams (2-20 chars) of title tokens
- content_prefix: edge n-grams (2-20 chars) of content tokens
- preview: pre-truncated content (~200 chars) for quick display

Key implementation details:

1. Edge N-gram Generation
   - generate_edge_ngrams() splits text on non-alphanumeric boundaries
   - Produces all prefixes from 2 to min(20, word_length) characters
   - Enables "cal" to match "calculate", "entr" to match "entropy"

2. Schema Changes
   - Bump SCHEMA_VERSION to "v4" and SCHEMA_HASH to include "edge-ngram-preview"
   - title_prefix/content_prefix: indexed but not stored (save space)
   - preview: stored for fast retrieval without parsing full content
   - created_at: add FAST flag for efficient range filtering

3. Preview Field
   - build_preview() truncates to max_chars with ellipsis
   - Stored in index for sub-millisecond snippet retrieval
   - Avoids expensive SnippetGenerator for prefix-only queries

The schema hash change will trigger automatic index rebuild on first
run, ensuring all existing data gets the new edge n-gram tokenization.

Part of P12 (Ultra-low-latency search v2) initiative.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
